content: If we expand the Merriam-Webster definition of an agent as "any entity that acts to produce an effect" to include non-artificial biological and chemical agents, we can hypothesize that naturally evolving biological pathogens (a class of agent per Wikipedia) behave as if they use an ICM-like curiosity mechanism to drive their exploration of new host niches. Natural selection implicitly rewards these biological agents for discovering novel, unoccupied host environments the same way ICM rewards artificial autonomous agents for exploring unseen state spaces, shaping their mutation-driven adaptation.
